int.description: 32-bit signed two's complement integer, range -2^31 to 2^31-1

long.description: 64-bit signed two's complement integer for large whole numbers

boolean.description: Logical type with exactly two values: true and false

char.description: 16-bit unsigned Unicode character type (UTF-16 code unit)

byte.description: 8-bit signed two's complement integer, range -128 to 127

void.description: Indicates a method returns no value; corresponding wrapper is java.lang.Void

String.signature: public final class String implements Serializable, Comparable<String>, CharSequence
String.description: Immutable sequence of characters, the most commonly used class in Java

Object.description: Root of the class hierarchy; every class has Object as a superclass

List.description: Ordered collection that allows duplicate elements and positional access

Map.description: Collection of key-value pairs with unique keys; HashMap and TreeMap are common implementations

Optional.description: Container that may or may not contain a non-null value, alternative to null

Stream.signature: public interface Stream<T> extends BaseStream<T, Stream<T>>
Stream.description: Sequence of elements supporting sequential and parallel aggregate operations

CompletableFuture.signature: public class CompletableFuture<T> implements Future<T>, CompletionStage<T>
CompletableFuture.description: Future that can be explicitly completed, supporting chained asynchronous operations

var.description: Local variable type inference, actual type deduced from the initializer expression
